family: Fries-, Photo-Fries, and Anionic Ortho-Fries Rearrangement
rationale: The Fries rearrangement of phenyl acetate to 2-hydroxyacetophenone is the simplest and most frequently cited textbook example of this reaction family. It clearly demonstrates the core transformation: the intramolecular rearrangement of an acyl group from a phenolic oxygen to an ortho position on the aromatic ring, catalyzed by a Lewis acid (AlCl3). This example is minimal, acyclic in its core functional groups, and avoids complex substituents.
collision_avoidance_note: This example uses the simplest possible aromatic system (phenyl) and acyl group (acetyl) to avoid overlap with more complex aromatic substitution reactions. It avoids motifs like polycyclic arenes, diazo groups, or other reactive functionalities that could trigger unrelated named reaction queries. The focus is strictly on the acyl group migration characteristic of the Fries rearrangement.
